compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
G3 container coverage review (parent 3fe1 + this exemplar child): clean — no findings.

This child fully and uniquely covers parent AC2 (exemplar package llm/plan_review/ fully remediated as the reference others copy; BLE001 + scoped T201 enabled with a shrinking per-file-ignores allowlist; plan_review excluded). It also materially contributes to SC1 (establishes the one lint-enforced way via the reference PR + lint enable) and SC3 (turns on the CI gate so new unguarded blind-excepts / stray prints fail). It sets the narrowing-with-test pattern relevant to AC4.

4-bucket map vs parent criteria for this pairing:
- AC1 (substrate + stdout-purity test): structural — covered by sibling 8fbd.
- AC2 (exemplar + enable BLE001/T201 + shrinking allowlist): FULLY by this child.
- AC3 (~100 sites in batches): partial (plan_review subset); remainder covered by sweeps 8d01/d360/ceaa/fd72/0569.
- AC4 (narrowing bug + regression test): partial (sets pattern with test); also ceaa + sweeps.
- AC5 (allowlist emptied + final gate): partial (enables/seeds allowlist; emptying covered by 0ec1).
- SC1: fully (this child + 0ec1). SC2: partial (8fbd + sweeps). SC3: fully (gate enabled; finalized by 0ec1).

No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered when checked against the whole sibling roster; no coverage gap is attributable to this pairing.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
G4 child-consistency review (parent 3fe1 + this exemplar child; whole sibling roster considered). This child is the rule-enabler/allowlist-author of the epic — it turns on BLE001 + scoped T201 and authors the per-file-ignores allowlist "covering all un-swept packages." That allowlist is implicit shared state for the whole roster (each sweep removes its package; close-out 0ec1 empties it), so this pairing is where the allowlist's *initial completeness* is contracted. Two high-confidence cross-child findings; modes otherwise clean (see end).

---
FINDING 1 — Dependency gap / scope coverage: this child's own "CI is green" AC and the allowlist it authors do not have an owner for `src/rebar/_io/`, a package that carries gated BLE001 sites and is in NO sweep's file_impact.

criteria: [this child AC3 "pyproject.toml enables BLE001 + T201 with a per-file-ignores allowlist covering all un-swept packages; CI is green"; this child What "an allowlist that exempts every not-yet-swept package"; parent AC5 "The allowlist is emptied; make lint + the full test suite pass"; parent SC3 "future joe-janitor audit finds no unguarded blind-except cluster"]
location: src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py:225,237,243,258,271,276,289 (7 `except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001` sites) vs. the file_impact of every sweep sibling.
evidence:
  - Sweep partition by file_impact (read from each ticket): 8d01 {__init__.py, reducer/, graph/, _store/}; d360 {grounding/, _engine_support/}; ceaa {mcp_server.py, llm/workflow/, llm/ top-level}; fd72 {_cli/, _commands/}; 0569 {_engine/rebar_reconciler/}; 16a1 {llm/plan_review/}.
  - `src/rebar/_io/` is in NONE of those file_impacts (directory listing confirms a distinct `_io/` package exists; grep confirms 7 pre-seeded `# noqa: BLE001` sites in import_ndjson.py, several over a best-effort body).
  - Cross-checked the other top-level un-swept files for absence: `src/rebar/_engine.py` and `src/rebar/config.py` have 0 `except Exception` (grep) — so they are NOT a coverage hole. `_io/` is the one verified-unowned package with gated sites.
scenarios:
  - For THIS child's AC3 "CI is green" to hold at enable-time, this child's allowlist MUST enumerate `_io/` (it is un-swept and holds BLE001 sites) — the "exempts every not-yet-swept package" rule covers it IF the author treats `_io/` as a package needing exemption. That is a live correctness obligation on this pairing: omit `_io/` from the authored allowlist and this child's own CI-green AC fails the moment BLE001 turns on.
  - Downstream: because no sweep owns `_io/`, it never leaves the allowlist, so close-out 0ec1's "empty the allowlist" is unreachable from within the planned roster. This pairing seeds the gap (authors the allowlist whose only exit path for `_io/` is undefined).
impact: A package coverage hole rooted at the allowlist this child authors: `_io/` (≥7 BLE001 sites) must be in the initial allowlist for this child's CI-green AC, yet has no owning sweep to ever bring it to the floor and remove it. Absence cross-checked against the whole roster — confirmed no sibling lists `_io/` / `import_ndjson.py` in file_impact.
checklist_item: dependency gap; scope coverage
suggested_fix: (a) Explicitly enumerate `src/rebar/_io/` in the initial allowlist this child authors (so this child's CI-green AC is actually satisfiable), and (b) assign `_io/` to an owning sweep (e.g. 8d01 / d360 or a new "remaining leaves" batch) with a `blocks` edge into close-out 0ec1, so the package can be brought to the floor and the allowlist eventually emptied.

---
FINDING 2 — Conflicting assumptions: the scoped-T201 contract this child ENABLES ("allowed in the CLI presentation layer `_cli`/`_commands`; banned in library/core/MCP") collides with 42 pre-existing `# noqa: T201` sites that live in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` — library/core, not the CLI presentation layer.

criteria: [this child What "Enable ruff T201 (scoped) ... T201 scoping: banned in library/core/MCP; allowed in the CLI presentation layer (_cli, _commands) via per-file-ignore"; this child AC3 "allowlist covering all un-swept packages; CI is green"; sibling fd72 AC "The T201 per-file-ignore covers the CLI presentation layer"; sibling 0569 (reconciler sweep)]
location: 42 `# noqa: T201` sites in src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/ (reconcile.py:285..1280, applier.py, batch_dispatch.py, apply_inbound.py, outbound_differ.py, acli.py, invariants.py — grep confirmed). The reconciler is under `_engine/`, NOT under `_cli`/`_commands`.
evidence:
  - This child's only named T201 carve-out is the CLI presentation layer (`_cli`, `_commands`). The reconciler is a separate, library/core subprocess package with 42 print sites already annotated `# noqa: T201`.
  - For THIS child's "CI is green" at enable-time, those 42 reconciler T201 sites must be covered — but the *only* T201 allow this child names is the CLI presentation layer. So the reconciler can only stay green via the un-swept-package allowlist exemption (BLE001+T201 allowlist must also exempt `rebar_reconciler/` for T201). That is consistent ONLY if this child's authored allowlist actually scopes T201 (not just BLE001) for `rebar_reconciler/`. The child's What couples the allowlist to "BLE001 and T201" but its example carve-out language ("allowed in the CLI presentation layer") risks an author treating T201 as needing only the CLI per-file-ignore + no reconciler exemption — which would red-CI the reconciler at enable-time.
scenarios:
  - Author enables T201, adds the CLI per-file-ignore, but does not also put `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` in the (un-swept) T201 allowlist: the 42 pre-existing reconciler prints now violate T201 even WITH their `# noqa: T201` only if those noqas are themselves stale — but regardless, the reconciler is un-swept and must be allowlisted; if the allowlist is BLE001-only and not T201, this child's CI-green AC fails on the reconciler.
  - Note: the deeper reconciler dispute (migrate-vs-keep prints, handler-less `rebar_reconciler.*` namespace) is owned by 0569 / close-out 0ec1 and is already filed there — NOT re-attributed to this pairing. The finding here is narrowly that THIS child enables the scoped rule whose only documented T201 allow is the CLI layer, while a library/core package (`rebar_reconciler/`) carries 42 T201 sites that this child's allowlist must (also) scope for T201 to keep enable-time CI green.
impact: The scoped-T201 enablement this child authors is under-specified for the un-swept reconciler package: unless this child's allowlist applies to T201 (not only BLE001) for `_engine/rebar_reconciler/`, turning T201 on red-CIs 42 sites, contradicting this child's own AC3 "CI is green."
checklist_item: conflicting assumptions
suggested_fix: Make explicit in this child's What/AC that the authored per-file-ignores allowlist scopes BOTH BLE001 and T201 for every un-swept package (name `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` as a T201-and-BLE001 allowlist entry), distinct from the *permanent* CLI-presentation-layer T201 per-file-ignore. Add an enable-time check that `ruff` is clean across the repo immediately after enabling, with the reconciler covered by the un-swept allowlist (not by the CLI carve-out).

---
Modes checked and clean for this pairing: implicit shared state (this child authors the allowlist; sweeps mutate it later behind `blocks` edges from 16a1 → each sweep → 0ec1, so no concurrent writer at author-time); scope overlap (exemplar remediation of `llm/plan_review/` is uniquely this child's; no sibling touches that package — confirmed plan_review except/print sites are all in-package); ordering violation (the "land before the sweeps, lock the lint first" ordering is correctly encoded: 8fbd blocks 16a1; 16a1 blocks 8d01/fd72/d360/ceaa/0569; chain is consistent); consumer impact (the stdout/JSON-RPC data contract is owned by 8fbd's purity test + fd72's CLI carve-out, not regressed by this exemplar package, which has no stdout data prints — grep shows plan_review `print(` hits are all `material_fingerprint`, no real prints); residual references (no dangling reference introduced by this pairing). The two findings above are the dependency-gap/scope-coverage and conflicting-assumptions modes.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
G4 child-consistency review (parent 3fe1 + this exemplar child; whole sibling roster considered). This child is the rule-enabler/allowlist-author of the epic: it turns on BLE001 + scoped T201 in pyproject.toml and authors the per-file-ignores allowlist "covering all un-swept packages," and carries a "CI is green" AC at enable-time. That allowlist is implicit shared state for the whole roster (each sweep removes its package; close-out 0ec1 empties it), so this pairing is where the allowlist's initial completeness is contracted. Two high-confidence cross-child findings; other modes clean (see end).

---
FINDING 1 — Dependency gap / scope coverage: this child's "CI is green" AC and the allowlist it authors have no owner for src/rebar/_io/, a package that carries gated BLE001 sites and is in NO sweep's file_impact.

criteria: [this child AC3 "pyproject.toml enables BLE001 + T201 with a per-file-ignores allowlist covering all un-swept packages; CI is green"; this child What "an allowlist that exempts every not-yet-swept package"; parent AC5 "The allowlist is emptied; make lint + the full test suite pass"; parent SC3 "future joe-janitor audit finds no unguarded blind-except cluster"]
location: src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py:225,237,243,258,271,276,289 (7 `except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001` sites) vs. the file_impact of every sweep sibling.
evidence:
  - Sweep partition by file_impact (read from each ticket): 8d01 {__init__.py, reducer/, graph/, _store/}; d360 {grounding/, _engine_support/}; ceaa {mcp_server.py, llm/workflow/, llm/ top-level}; fd72 {_cli/, _commands/}; 0569 {_engine/rebar_reconciler/}; this child {llm/plan_review/}.
  - `src/rebar/_io/` is in NONE of those file_impacts (directory listing confirms a distinct `_io/` package; grep confirms the 7 pre-seeded `# noqa: BLE001` sites above, several over a best-effort body).
  - Cross-checked other top-level un-swept files for absence: `src/rebar/_engine.py` and `src/rebar/config.py` have 0 `except Exception` (grep) — so they are NOT a hole. `_io/` is the one verified-unowned package with gated sites.
scenarios:
  - For THIS child's AC3 "CI is green" to hold the moment BLE001 turns on, this child's authored allowlist MUST enumerate `_io/` (un-swept, holds BLE001 sites). The "exempts every not-yet-swept package" rule covers it only if the author treats `_io/` as a package needing exemption — a live correctness obligation on this pairing: omit `_io/` and this child's own CI-green AC fails at enable-time.
  - Downstream: because no sweep owns `_io/`, it never leaves the allowlist, so close-out 0ec1's "empty the allowlist" is unreachable from within the planned roster. This pairing seeds the gap (authors the allowlist whose only exit path for `_io/` is undefined).
impact: A package coverage hole rooted at the allowlist this child authors: `_io/` (>=7 BLE001 sites) must be in the initial allowlist for this child's CI-green AC, yet has no owning sweep to bring it to the floor and remove it. Absence cross-checked against the whole roster — confirmed no sibling lists `_io/` / `import_ndjson.py` in file_impact.
checklist_item: dependency gap; scope coverage
suggested_fix: (a) Explicitly enumerate `src/rebar/_io/` in the initial allowlist this child authors (so its CI-green AC is satisfiable), and (b) assign `_io/` to an owning sweep (e.g. 8d01 / d360 or a new "remaining leaves" batch) with a `blocks` edge into close-out 0ec1, so the package can be brought to the floor and the allowlist eventually emptied.

---
FINDING 2 — Conflicting assumptions: the scoped-T201 contract this child ENABLES ("allowed in the CLI presentation layer `_cli`/`_commands`; banned in library/core/MCP") collides with ~42 pre-existing `# noqa: T201` sites that live in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` — a library/core subprocess package, NOT the CLI presentation layer.

criteria: [this child What "Enable ruff T201 (scoped) ... banned in library/core/MCP; allowed in the CLI presentation layer (_cli, _commands) via per-file-ignore"; this child AC3 "allowlist covering all un-swept packages; CI is green"; sibling fd72 (CLI T201 carve-out); sibling 0569 (reconciler sweep)]
location: ~42 `# noqa: T201` sites in src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/ (reconcile.py:285..1280, applier.py, batch_dispatch.py, apply_inbound.py, outbound_differ.py, acli.py, invariants.py — grep confirmed). The reconciler is under `_engine/`, NOT under `_cli`/`_commands`.
evidence:
  - This child's only named permanent T201 carve-out is the CLI presentation layer (`_cli`, `_commands`). The reconciler is a separate library/core subprocess package with ~42 print sites already annotated `# noqa: T201`.
  - For THIS child's "CI is green" at enable-time, those reconciler T201 sites can stay green ONLY via the un-swept-package allowlist exemption — i.e. this child's authored allowlist must scope T201 (not only BLE001) for `rebar_reconciler/`. The child's What couples the allowlist to "BLE001 and T201" but its example carve-out language ("allowed in the CLI presentation layer") risks an author treating T201 as needing only the CLI per-file-ignore + no reconciler exemption, which red-CIs the reconciler at enable-time.
scenarios:
  - Author enables T201, adds the CLI per-file-ignore, but does not put `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` in the (un-swept) T201 allowlist: the reconciler is un-swept and must be allowlisted; if the authored allowlist is BLE001-only and not T201, this child's CI-green AC fails on the reconciler.
  - Note: the deeper reconciler dispute (migrate-vs-keep prints, handler-less `rebar_reconciler.*` namespace, the `^ERROR:` grep contract) is owned by 0569 / close-out 0ec1 and is already filed there — NOT re-attributed to this pairing. The finding here is narrowly that THIS child enables the scoped rule whose only documented T201 allow is the CLI layer, while a library/core package (`rebar_reconciler/`) carries ~42 T201 sites that this child's allowlist must also scope for T201 to keep enable-time CI green.
impact: The scoped-T201 enablement this child authors is under-specified for the un-swept reconciler package: unless the authored allowlist applies to T201 (not only BLE001) for `_engine/rebar_reconciler/`, turning T201 on red-CIs ~42 sites, contradicting this child's own AC3 "CI is green."
checklist_item: conflicting assumptions
suggested_fix: Make explicit in this child's What/AC that the authored per-file-ignores allowlist scopes BOTH BLE001 and T201 for every un-swept package (name `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` as a T201-and-BLE001 allowlist entry), distinct from the permanent CLI-presentation-layer T201 per-file-ignore. Add an enable-time check that `ruff` is clean across the repo immediately after enabling, with the reconciler covered by the un-swept allowlist (not by the CLI carve-out).

---
Modes checked and clean for this pairing: implicit shared state (this child authors the allowlist; sweeps mutate it later behind `blocks` edges 16a1 -> each sweep -> 0ec1, so no concurrent writer at author-time); scope overlap (exemplar remediation of `llm/plan_review/` is uniquely this child's — grep confirms all of that package's ~30 except sites and the seeded BLE001 noqas are in-package; no sibling touches it); ordering violation (the "land before the sweeps, lock the lint first" ordering is correctly encoded: 8fbd blocks 16a1; 16a1 blocks 8d01/fd72/d360/ceaa/0569; chain consistent); consumer impact (the stdout/JSON-RPC data contract is owned by 8fbd's purity test + fd72's CLI carve-out; this exemplar package has NO stdout/stderr data prints — grep shows zero real `print(` and zero `file=sys.stderr` in plan_review/, only `material_fingerprint` substring matches — so AC2 "migrate stderr-diagnostic prints" is vacuously satisfied, benign, not a contradiction); residual references (no dangling reference introduced by this pairing). The two findings above are the dependency-gap/scope-coverage and conflicting-assumptions modes.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — edit-set / scope accuracy (G1G2). Two coaching findings + affirmations. (Note: I see the parent epic's plan-review already corrected the T201/reconciler scoping; these findings are distinct from that and concern the named edit-set in *this* child.)

**Findings**

- [ ] **`_cli` is named as a legitimate-`print` T201 surface, but it has zero `print()` calls — the real stdout-contract prints live in `_commands/`.** The T201-scoping bullet lists "(a) the CLI presentation layer (`_cli`, `_commands`), where `print(json.dumps(...))` stdout output is the machine contract" as needing a per-file-ignore. Verified: `src/rebar/_cli/` contains NO `print(` statements — it emits via `sys.stdout.write(_json.dumps(...))` throughout (`_cli/__init__.py:139`, `_cli/_llm_commands.py:54/65/113/157/...`, `_cli/_workflow_commands.py:179/205/...`); the only `print(` token in `_cli` is a comment at `_cli/__init__.py:327`. A T201 per-file-ignore for `_cli` would therefore be a dead/no-op allowlist entry. The actual `print(json.dumps(...))` machine-contract surface that T201 would flag is `src/rebar/_commands/` (`composer.py:244`, `show_config.py:107`, `session_log.py:187`, etc.) — already correctly named. Since this child's diff "IS the convention every other batch copies," a phantom `_cli` entry risks propagating into sibling fd72's CLI carve-out.
  - criteria: G1G2
  - location: description §What → T201-scoping bullet, "(a) the CLI presentation layer (`_cli`, `_commands`)"
  - evidence: `search_files "print(" src/rebar/_cli` → 1 hit, a comment (`_cli/__init__.py:327`); the data emitters use `sys.stdout.write(_json.dumps(...))`. `search_files "print(" src/rebar/_commands` → many real `print(json.dumps(...))` sites (`composer.py:244`, `show_config.py:107`, `session_log.py:187`).
  - suggested_fix: Drop `_cli` from the T201 legitimate-print list (keep `_commands/` as the stdout-contract surface). If `_cli` is intended to be T201-clean-by-construction via `sys.stdout.write`, state that explicitly so fd72 doesn't add a no-op `_cli` per-file-ignore.

- [ ] **The "migrate stderr-diagnostic prints in the package" step / AC #2 has no targets in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/`.** What-bullet 2 ("Migrate `print(..., file=sys.stderr)` diagnostics in the package to the named logger") and AC #2 ("Stderr-diagnostic prints in the package are migrated to `logging.getLogger(__name__)`") have no matching edit target: the package contains zero `print(` calls and zero `file=sys.stderr` (the only `print(`-substring hits are the `material_fingerprint` function name). The package's broad-except sites swallow via `return False`/`return 0`/typed-result paths, not stderr prints. For the *reference* PR, an AC with no corresponding hunk is worth making non-vacuous so copiers don't infer a migration step that isn't demonstrated.
  - criteria: G1G2
  - location: description §What bullet 2 + Acceptance Criteria line 2
  - evidence: ABSENCE — `search_files "print(" src/rebar/llm/plan_review` → only `material_fingerprint`/`current_material_fingerprint`; `search_files "file=sys.stderr" src/rebar/llm/plan_review` → no matches. Broad-except sites e.g. `sidecar.py:44`, `sidecar.py:89` `return`/`return 0`.
  - suggested_fix: Either drop the print-migration What-bullet + AC #2 (the package has no stderr prints to migrate — say so, mirroring how parent AC4 was made explicitly non-vacuous), or re-scope it if a logger-emit demonstration is wanted in the exemplar regardless.

**Affirmations (G1G2 criteria this chunk satisfies)**
- Every concrete file/dir named EXISTS: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` (9 modules incl. `sidecar.py`, `det_floor.py`, `orchestrator.py`, `registry.py`, `sizing.py`), `pyproject.toml` `[tool.ruff.lint]` (line 178, `select = ["E","F","W","I","UP","B"]`), `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` (the real shrinking-allowlist/strangler pattern, lines 11–17), and `_engine/rebar_reconciler/`.
- The load-bearing claim "all 42 existing `# noqa: T201` sites live [in the reconciler], NOT in the CLI" is VERIFIED EXACTLY: `search_files "# noqa: T201"` → 42 hits, every one under `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/` (acli/applier/outbound_differ/batch_dispatch/apply_inbound/reconcile/invariants); none in `_cli`/`_commands`/`plan_review`.
- The broad-except edit set is real: 28 `except Exception` sites across the package (mix of bare and pre-seeded `# noqa: BLE001`), giving the floor remediation a concrete, in-package target set — no hallucinated targets.
- Consumer/ordering enumeration is sound: the lint-enablement consumers (pyproject + the un-swept-package allowlist) are named, `plan_review/` is correctly excluded from the allowlist, and the "land before the sweeps, lock the lint first" ordering is encoded via `blocks` edges (16a1 → 8d01/fd72/d360/ceaa/0569). High blast radius is acknowledged, not hidden.
- Behavioral classification: all hunks are IN-scope remediation (logging/noqa) + config enablement (BLE001/T201); no CREATION/new-behavior hunk detected.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — [T3] Integration feasibility (overlay): PASS, no findings.

The only external integration this plan asserts is **ruff** (enable BLE001 + scoped T201, add a `per-file-ignores` allowlist). This is an internal, already-used integration — the overlay leans toward not-applicable — but I verified every asserted capability against the codebase and all check out:

- (a) technical_feasibility — ACHIEVABLE, no capability gap. ruff is already pinned (`ruff==0.15.19`, pyproject.toml:106) and wired through `make lint` + CI (Makefile:28; .github/workflows/test.yml:50). `[tool.ruff.lint]` already exists with `select = ["E","F","W","I","UP","B"]` (pyproject.toml:178-179); adding `BLE001`/`T201` to `select` plus a `per-file-ignores` table is standard, well-supported ruff config.
- (b) named rules/config keys exist — MATCH. `BLE001` and `T201` are real ruff codes already recognized in-tree (95+ `# noqa: BLE001` and 42 `# noqa: T201` annotations present and effective). `per-file-ignores` is a documented ruff config key. The shrinking-allowlist exemplar `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` exists (one-path-per-line, lines 11-17) and matches the pattern the plan clones. The plan's load-bearing claim that "all 42 existing `# noqa: T201` sites live [in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/`], NOT in the CLI" is verified EXACTLY — every T201 hit is under `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/`; none in `_cli`/`_commands`/`plan_review`.
- (c) auth/HTTPS preconditions — correctly N/A (local linter, no network/credentials).
- (d) no critical capability gap → no SPIKE required.

Affirmations (T3): integration achievable as described; named rule codes + `per-file-ignores` key verified present (MATCH); auth/HTTPS correctly not-applicable; no capability gap routing to a SPIKE.

(Scope note, not a T3 finding and already raised by the G1G2/G4 passes: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` has zero real `print(`/`file=sys.stderr` sites, so What-bullet 2 / AC #2's print-migration step has no in-package target. Out of scope for T3 — flagged only for cross-reference.)

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: PASS 1 (G1G2 — edit-set / scope accuracy): One finding — the stderr-print migration target appears absent in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/`. Grep for `file=sys.stderr` and `sys.stderr` returns zero matches in the package; the only `print(` hits are the `material_fingerprint` symbol name, not print calls. All other named targets verified present (package + broad-except sites, `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt`, all 42 `# noqa: T201` sites confirmed in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` and none in CLI, `_cli`/`_commands` dirs exist).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Exemplar landed (commit 46d1abee). Plan-review PASS (0 blocking, 8 advisory). Advisory triage: (fa421, VALID/critical) _engine_support added to T201 allowlist — it carries print(json.dumps) stdout contract; without it CI would break. (fc67, VALID) added proving test tests/unit/test_error_handling_convention.py using ruff --stdin-filename to assert BLE001/T201 FIRE on a plan_review path and are SUPPRESSED on an allowlisted path. (f75a+f6d7, VALID) AC#2 'migrate stderr prints' is non-applicable: plan_review had ZERO print/sys.stderr sites (already logger-based) — recorded so AC#2 isn't vacuous. (fd746, VALID-minor) 41 (not 42) noqa:T201 sites, all under _engine/rebar_reconciler — allowlisted by directory so count doesn't affect scoping. (f125+fa80, discretionary) opportunistic narrowing is optional for the exemplar; demonstrated noqa+log forms instead, narrowing-with-test deferred to the core-leaves sweep (_json_or). (f21b, resolved) the logging-substrate prerequisite is 8fbd (closed). Discovered+fixed: ruff per-file-ignore globs match * across /, so a naive llm/*.py glob would wrongly exempt plan_review — enumerated llm top-level files explicitly instead (proven gated by the test). Code-review subagent: clean. make lint green; full suite 3204 passed/12 skipped.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification: PASS

All five acceptance criteria are met by the implementation in the repository.

### AC1 — All broad-except sites meet the observability floor
**MET.** Every `except Exception` site in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` carries a `# noqa: BLE001` with a justification reason string. 29 sites verified across `sidecar.py`, `registry.py`, `__init__.py`, `det_floor.py`, `orchestrator.py`, `attest.py`, and `sizing.py`. Sites annotated "broad-but-logged" also have a corresponding `logger.warning(..., exc_info=True)` call. No unjustified `except ...: pass` remains.

### AC2 — Stderr-diagnostic prints migrated to `logging.getLogger(__name__)`
**MET (vacuously).** `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` contains zero `print(` calls and zero `file=sys.stderr` references — the package was already fully logger-based. `logging.getLogger(__name__)` is defined in 5 modules (`sidecar.py`, `__init__.py`, `det_floor.py`, `orchestrator.py`, `attest.py`). The landing comment explicitly records this non-applicability.

### AC3 — `pyproject.toml` enables BLE001+T201 with a shrinking per-file-ignores allowlist
**MET.** `pyproject.toml` line 184: `select = ["E", "F", "W", "I", "UP", "B", "BLE001", "T201"]`. A complete `[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]` table (lines 202–234) exempts all un-swept packages: `__init__.py`, `reducer/*`, `graph/*`, `_store/*`, `_io/*` (T201 only; BLE001 sites carry inline noqas), `grounding/*`, `_engine_support/*`, `mcp_server.py`, `llm/` top-level files (enumerated explicitly), `_cli/*`, `_commands/*`, `_engine/rebar_reconciler/*`, `_engine/jira-capability-probe.py`, and `tests/**`.

### AC4 — `llm/plan_review/` excluded from the allowlist
**MET.** `plan_review` does not appear anywhere in `pyproject.toml`. Lines 219–221 explicitly explain why: ruff per-file-ignore globs match `*` across `/`, so llm top-level files are enumerated individually to ensure `plan_review/` stays gated. The proving test `tests/unit/test_error_handling_convention.py` (all three test functions) directly validates this: `test_exemplar_path_is_gated` asserts BLE001+T201 fire on `plan_review/` paths, `test_allowlisted_path_is_suppressed` confirms the allowlist works elsewhere, and `test_exemplar_package_is_clean` verifies the real package is already conformant.

### AC5 — `make lint` + full test suite pass
**MET per evidence.** The landing comment (commit 46d1abee) records: "make lint green; full suite 3204 passed/12 skipped." The proving test file `tests/unit/test_error_handling_convention.py` exists and is non-trivial (3 live test functions using `subprocess.run` + ruff `--stdin-filename` to gate BLE001/T201 behaviour by path).

**Verdict: PASS** — every acceptance criterion stated by the ticket is demonstrably satisfied by the implementation.

compiled_state.description:
# Exemplar remediation: llm/plan_review/ end-to-end + enable BLE001/T201 with shrinking allowlist

The reference PR. Its diff IS the convention — every other batch copies it. Do NOT parallelize this with
the sweeps; it must land (and lock the lint) before they start. Depends on the logging substrate.

## What
- Remediate every broad-except in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` to the observability floor: log with
  `exc_info` OR carry a justified `# noqa: BLE001` (reason string required). Opportunistic narrowing only
  where a specific type is genuinely expected, with a test.
- Migrate `print(..., file=sys.stderr)` diagnostics in the package to the named logger.
- Enable ruff **BLE001** and **T201** (scoped) in `pyproject.toml` `[tool.ruff.lint]`, gated behind a
  `per-file-ignores` allowlist that exempts every not-yet-swept package (clone the shrinking-allowlist
  pattern from `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt`); `llm/plan_review/` is NOT in the allowlist.
- T201 scoping (corrected per plan-review G4): banned in library/core/MCP, but a `per-file-ignore` is needed
  for the legitimate-`print` surfaces — BOTH (a) the CLI presentation layer (`_cli`, `_commands`), where
  `print(json.dumps(...))` stdout output is the machine contract, AND (b) the reconciler subprocess
  (`_engine/rebar_reconciler/`), which legitimately prints operational `RECON:`/`ERROR:` lines to its OWN
  stderr (all 42 existing `# noqa: T201` sites live there, NOT in the CLI).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All broad-except sites in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` meet the observability floor; no unjustified
      `except ...: pass` remains.
- [ ] Stderr-diagnostic prints in the package are migrated to `logging.getLogger(__name__)`.
- [ ] `pyproject.toml` enables `BLE001` + `T201` with a `per-file-ignores` allowlist covering all un-swept
      packages; CI is green.
- [ ] `llm/plan_review/` is excluded from the allowlist (it is fully conformant).
- [ ] `make lint` + full test suite pass.

enable ruff BLE001+T201 with shrinking per-file-ignores allowlist; plan_review NOT exempt
